messages.content: You are an AI research assistant specialized in designing research methods and experimental plans.

<user_request>
Design a computational method for modeling ultra-long genomic sequences (100kb+) at base-pair resolution to capture distal promoter-enhancer interactions. Current transformer models have O(n^2) attention complexity limiting them to 10kb. Linear state-space models (Mamba, Hyena, RWKV) show promise but have not been validated on genomic regulatory tasks. The method must work with limited labeled functional genomic data (MPRA, STARR-seq), integrate 3D chromatin conformation data (Hi-C, Micro-C), and predict gene expression, variant effects (eQTLs), and distal enhancer-gene links.

<think>
Problem: Enhancers can be 100kb-1Mb from promoters. Enformer/Borzoi use CNNs with limited receptive fields requiring downsampling. Linear SSMs have not been benchmarked. 3D chromatin data integration remains challenging. Limited label data.
Method Goal: Model 100kb+ sequences at base resolution, capture distal regulatory interactions with limited labeled data.
Experiment Goal: Evaluate on gene expression prediction (GTEx), variant effect prediction (eQTL fine-mapping), distal enhancer-gene linking (HiChIP benchmarks).
</think>
You are an AI research assistant specialized in designing research methods and experimental plans.

<user_request>
Design a multi-modal integration method for unpaired single-cell multi-omics data combining scRNA-seq, scATAC-seq, and scHi-C from the same tissue but different cells. The method must handle severe batch effects between modalities, fundamentally different feature spaces (count vs. binary peak vs. 3D contact matrix), preserve fine-grained cell-type identity without over-correction, and infer cross-modality regulatory relationships including enhancer-gene linking at single-cell resolution. Current tools like Seurat v5 and scVI require paired data or blur cell-type distinctions.

<think>
Problem: Multi-omics unpaired data have different structures, severe batch effects, and heterogeneous feature spaces. Existing methods need paired data or over-correct. Enhancer-gene linking at single-cell resolution unsolved. scHi-C scalability challenge.
Method Goal: Integrate unpaired scRNA+scATAC+scHi-C preserving cell-type identity while enabling cross-modality regulatory inference.
Experiment Goal: Evaluate on cell-type clustering (ARI, NMI), cross-modality prediction, enhancer-gene linking against ChIP-seq and promoter-capture Hi-C gold standards.
</think>
You are an AI research assistant specialized in designing research methods and experimental plans.

<user_request>
Design a deep learning method that predicts 3D structural changes induced by post-translational modifications (PTMs) including phosphorylation, glycosylation, ubiquitination, and acetylation without requiring full molecular dynamics simulation. AlphaFold3 and similar tools treat modified residues as standard amino acids, ignoring the significant conformational effects of PTMs. The method should incorporate PTM-specific physicochemical constraints into structure prediction, handle scarce PTM structural data in the PDB, and generalize to PTM types not seen in training.

<think>
Problem: PTMs critically regulate protein function but AlphaFold ignores them. Modified residues have different chemistry affecting structure. MD is too slow for proteome scale. PDB has limited PTM data.
Method Goal: Predict PTM-induced structural changes without full MD using hybrid physics-based constraints with deep learning.
Experiment Goal: Evaluate on benchmark proteins with known PTM conformational changes (kinase phosphorylation, antibody glycosylation), comparison with MD ensemble RMSD, generalization to unseen PTM types.
</think>
You are an AI research assistant specialized in designing research methods and experimental plans.

<user_request>
Design a reference-free computational method for strain-level metagenomic binning that separates closely related bacterial strains (>99% ANI) from complex metagenomes using short-read sequencing. Current binning tools (MaxBin2, MetaBAT2, VAMB) use tetranucleotide frequency and coverage which cannot distinguish nearly identical strains. The method must exploit subtle SNP-level coverage variation patterns, handle strains at very different abundances (10:1 ratio), resolve antibiotic resistance gene carriage at the strain level, and work without reference genomes for target strains.

<think>
Problem: Strain-level binning is the frontier. Closely related strains share >99% ANI with near-identical composition. SNP-level coverage variation is the key signal but obscured by repeats, HGT, and conserved regions. Existing tools need reference genomes (StrainPhlAn) or have limited reference-free accuracy (DESMAN).
Method Goal: Reference-free strain-level binning exploiting subtle SNP and coverage variation patterns.
Experiment Goal: Evaluate on synthetic metagenomic communities (CAMI benchmarks with known strains), real human gut metagenomes with culture-based strain annotations, antibiotic resistance gene localization accuracy.
</think>
You are an AI research assistant specialized in designing research methods and experimental plans.

<user_request>
Design a zero-shot and few-shot drug-target interaction (DTI) prediction method based on 3D physicochemical complementarity between drug molecules and protein binding pockets. Current DTI methods (DeepDTA, GraphDTA, DrugBAN) fail on novel chemical scaffolds because they rely on molecular fingerprint similarity to known compounds. The cold-start problem means new chemical matter differs systematically from training data, and many proteins have no known ligands. The method must reason about shape complementarity, electrostatic matching, and hydrophobic contacts from first principles, handling both unseen drugs and unseen targets simultaneously.

<think>
Problem: Most DTI methods are transductive. Fingerprints encode substructure without 3D shape or electrostatics. Protein language models capture evolutionary info but not binding pocket geometry. True zero-shot needs 3D pharmacophore understanding.
Method Goal: Zero/few-shot DTI based on 3D physicochemical complementarity between drug pharmacophore and protein binding pocket.
Experiment Goal: Evaluate on scaffold-split benchmarks, unseen target class generalization (kinase to GPCR), few-shot scenarios (1-5 known binders), comparison with docking methods (AutoDock Vina, DiffDock).
</think>
You are an AI research assistant specialized in designing research methods and experimental plans.

<user_request>
Design a tissue-aware computational predictor of rare non-coding variant pathogenicity that accounts for tissue-specific regulatory logic. Over 90% of GWAS disease-associated hits are in non-coding regions, but existing predictors (CADD, DeepSEA, Enformer) have poor calibration for rare variants (MAF <0.1%), especially in tissue-specific enhancers and silencers. Coding variant predictors are mature because the genetic code provides a strong prior, but non-coding variants lack a universal code and their effects are highly context-dependent. The method should leverage deep learning models of regulatory grammar and incorporate tissue-specific TF binding and chromatin state.

<think>
Problem: Non-coding variants lack reliable pathogenicity predictors. Effects are tissue-specific and context-dependent. Current tools have limited annotation quality for rare cell types. DL models predict molecular phenotypes but are not clinically calibrated. MAVE data provides training signal for few loci.
Method Goal: Tissue-aware predictor of rare non-coding variant pathogenicity using regulatory grammar with clinical calibration.
Experiment Goal: Evaluate on ClinVar non-coding variants, MAVE benchmarks (MPRA, saturation mutagenesis), comparison with CADD/DeepSEA/Enformer at different AF thresholds, tissue-specific eQTL prediction (GTEx multi-tissue).
</think>
You are an AI research assistant specialized in designing research methods and experimental plans.

<user_request>
Design a boundary-aware spatial transcriptomics denoising method that imputes gene expression dropout events while preserving sharp spatial expression boundaries between tissue regions. Technologies like 10x Visium, MERFISH, and Slide-seq suffer from high dropout rates and spatial noise autocorrelation. Current methods either ignore spatial context (MAGIC, scImpute) or smooth too aggressively and blur real biological boundaries (BayesSpace, SpaGCN). The method must handle count-based noise models (negative binomial, zero-inflated), adaptively preserve edges at tissue interfaces, and scale to thousands of spatial spots and tens of thousands of genes.

<think>
Problem: Tension between leveraging spatial neighbors and preserving sharp boundaries. Gaussian processes assume smoothness. Graph methods have non-adaptive edge weights. DL treats denoising as reconstruction without boundary preservation. Count-based noise (NB/ZINB) not Gaussian.
Method Goal: Boundary-aware spatial denoising that imputes dropouts while preserving sharp spatial expression boundaries.
Experiment Goal: Evaluate on simulated data with ground truth, real spatial data with matched scRNA-seq reference (Slide-seq cerebellum, MERFISH MOp), improvement in spatial domain detection (ARI) and spatially variable gene detection (SPARK-X).
</think>
You are an AI research assistant specialized in designing research methods and experimental plans.

<user_request>
Design a gene regulatory network (GRN) inference method for non-model organisms that works with very limited expression data (10-50 RNA-seq samples), no TF binding motif databases, no ChIP-seq or ATAC-seq data, and no reference GRN from closely related species. Current GRN methods (GENIE3, GRNBoost2, SCENIC) require hundreds of samples, and orthology transfer fails when the closest model organism diverged >600 million years ago. The method should leverage deep evolutionary information from protein language models (ESM-2, ProtBERT), use phylogenetic conservation as a prior, and apply meta-learning across related clades.

<think>
Problem: GRN inference is underdetermined (n^2 edges with << n samples). Non-model organisms lack motif DBs, ChIP/ATAC, and known interactions. Cross-species transfer is hard due to rapid TFBS evolution and paralog divergence. Even rough GRNs are valuable for prioritizing experiments.
Method Goal: GRN inference for non-model organisms with limited data using deep evolutionary transfer learning.
Experiment Goal: Evaluate on model organisms with hidden data (Drosophila with 10 samples predicting FlyNet edges), cross-family transfer (vertebrate to invertebrate), real non-model species with qPCR validation for top edges.
</think>
You are an AI research assistant specialized in designing research methods and experimental plans.

<user_request>
Design a tissue-specific alternative splicing prediction method that integrates genomic sequence features with RNA-binding protein (RBP) expression profiles to predict percent-spliced-in (PSI) values across human tissues. Current splicing models (SpliceAI, Pangolin, AbSplice-DNA) predict the same pattern regardless of tissue type, but the same exon can be included in brain and skipped in liver. The method must model the interplay between cis-regulatory sequence elements and trans-acting RBP concentrations, condition predictions on tissue-specific RBP expression, and predict how RBP knockdown or tissue-specific genetic variants alter splicing outcomes.

<think>
Problem: >95% of human multi-exon genes are alternatively spliced. Tissue-specific splicing is RBP-driven. Current tools are tissue-agnostic. Cis-trans interplay is complex. GTEx has matched RNA-seq and RBP expression across 49 tissues. ENCODE eCLIP data for ~200 RBPs is available.
Method Goal: Tissue-specific splicing predictor integrating genomic sequence with RBP expression profiles.
Experiment Goal: Evaluate on GTEx tissue-specific PSI prediction, RBP knockdown effect prediction validated by eCLIP and shRNA RNA-seq, tissue-specific variant effect prediction (ClinVar splicing variants, Vex-seq).
</think>
You are an AI research assistant specialized in designing research methods and experimental plans.

<user_request>
Design a protein-protein interaction (PPI) interface prediction method that accounts for conformational flexibility, capable of predicting binding interfaces involving intrinsically disordered regions (IDRs) that fold upon binding and domains undergoing induced-fit conformational changes. AlphaFold-Multimer can predict some complexes but often fails when unbound conformations differ substantially from bound states, especially for kinase-substrate interactions. Current interface predictors (BIPSPI, DeepPPI, DSCRIPT, MaSIF) use static features and miss flexible interfaces. The method should efficiently sample conformational ensembles and scale for proteome-wide screening.

<think>
Problem: PPI interfaces involve flexibility — IDRs fold upon binding, domains undergo induced-fit, allostery transmits changes. AlphaFold-Multimer samples limited ensembles. MD is too slow. Surface representation learning assumes static surfaces.
Method Goal: PPI interface prediction accounting for conformational flexibility, handling IDRs and induced-fit binding.
Experiment Goal: Evaluate on flexible complex benchmarks (FlexBench, Dockground bound/unbound pairs), comparison with AlphaFold-Multimer on CASP-CAPRI, kinase-substrate pairs with known disordered interfaces (Kinase.com), ab initio prediction of fuzzy complexes.
</think>
